props.title: New Article: What to Do Before You Ask Your Community Anything

props.text: Governance teams face a structural trap: testing a proposal means surfacing it, and surfacing it changes the outcome. Traditional focus groups cost $7K–$20K per session and sell brand cover, not signal.

This piece looks at what a DAO gets when it runs a $1 MiroShark sim before posting to Snapshot — specifically the signed-result endpoint (PR #152) that lets a team publish a verifiable hash of the predicted community reaction before the vote is announced, turning "we tested this" from a claim into a proof.
